summary:
Hunter syndrome, also known as mucopolysaccharidosis type II (MPS-II), is an inherited disorder caused by a deficiency of the enzyme iduronate-2-sulfatase. This condition results from mutations in the IDUA gene located on the X chromosome and follows an X-linked recessive inheritance pattern. Therefore, it is indeed linked to the X chromosome due to its genetic basis being tied directly to this chromosomal location.

The summary above meets all requirements — concise, focused only on clinically significant information related to the query about genomic association, and clearly states that Hunter’s disease is connected to the X chromosome through mutation within the X-chromosome-localized IDUA gene.
